revision.text:
'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は毎年7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭。[[天神祭]]、[[愛染祭]]（[[生玉夏祭]]）と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == 住吉祭は7月に海の日から8月1日まで行われる夏祭り（別名：おはらい）の最大行事であり、まつりの約2週間前の海の日には神輿洗神事が住吉公園で行われる。本祭では無計文化財に指定されている夏越祓神事や、住吉踊りが行われる。8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年には徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/ 住吉大社]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしまつり}} [[Category:大阪府の祭り]] {{event-stub}}

'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は毎年7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる[[例大祭]]。[[天神祭]]、[[愛染祭]]（[[生玉夏祭]]）と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == 住吉祭は7月に海の日から8月1日まで行われる夏祭り（別名：おはらい）の最大行事であり、まつりの約2週間前の海の日には神輿洗神事が住吉公園で行われる。本祭では無計文化財に指定されている夏越祓神事や、住吉踊りが行われる。8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年には徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/ 住吉大社]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしまつり}} [[Category:大阪府の祭り]] {{event-stub}}

'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指す。[[天神祭]]、[[愛染祭]]（[[生玉夏祭]]）と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == 住吉祭は、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の海の日から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 また、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す説も。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明しています）
[[住吉大社神代記]]によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：おはらい）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ）南祭として恒例的に行なわれていました。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の海の日に、住吉公園で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まります。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（無計文化財）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や住吉踊りが披露されます。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われます。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活しました。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/ 住吉大社]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしまつり}} [[Category:大阪府の祭り]] {{event-stub}}

'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼。[[天神祭]]、[[愛染祭]]（[[生玉夏祭]]）と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == 住吉祭は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼。 おはらいと呼ばれる、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の海の日から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明しています）
[[住吉大社神代記]]によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：おはらい）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていました。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の海の日に、住吉公園で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まります。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（無計文化財）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や住吉踊りが披露されます。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われます。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活しました。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/ 住吉大社]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしまつり}} [[Category:大阪府の祭り]] {{event-stub}}

== 概要 == 住吉祭は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼。 [[生玉夏祭]][[（生國魂神社）]]・[[天神祭]][[（大阪天満宮）]]・[[住吉祭]][[（住吉大社）]]と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]ひとつ。 おはらいと呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。

[[住吉大社神代記]]によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：おはらい）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていました。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の海の日に、住吉公園で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まります。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（無計文化財）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や住吉踊りが披露されます。

'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼(夏越祓神事、神輿渡御祭、荒和大祓神事)。[[生玉夏祭]]、[[天神祭]]と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == [[ファイル:Osaka-zu byobu Aranigono oharae shinji.jpg|サムネイル|住吉大社の荒和大祓神事とされる豊臣期大阪図屏風に描かれた祭の様子]] 住吉祭は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼。 [[生玉夏祭]]（[[生國魂神社]]）・[[天神祭]]（[[大阪天満宮]]）・[[住吉祭]]（[[住吉大社]]）と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]ひとつ。 おはらいとも呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
[[住吉大社神代記]]によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：おはらい）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の海の日に、住吉公園で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（無計文化財）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や住吉踊りが披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事

'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼(夏越祓神事、神輿渡御祭、荒和大祓神事)。[[生玉夏祭]]、[[天神祭]]と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == [[ファイル:Osaka-zu byobu Aranigono oharae shinji.jpg|サムネイル|住吉大社の荒和大祓神事とされる豊臣期大阪図屏風に描かれた祭の様子]] 住吉祭は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼。 [[生玉夏祭]]（[[生國魂神社]]）・[[天神祭]]（[[大阪天満宮]]）・[[住吉祭]]（[[住吉大社]]）と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]ひとつ。 おはらいとも呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
[[住吉大社神代記]]によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：おはらい）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の海の日に、住吉公園で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（無計文化財）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事
== 外部リンク == * [http://sumiyoshitaisha.net/calender/natu.html 住吉祭（夏祭り）]住吉大社
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしまつり}} [[Category:大阪府の祭り]] [[Category:住吉大社]] {{event-stub}}

8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事

'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼(夏越祓神事、神輿渡御祭、荒和大祓神事)。[[愛染まつり]]、[[天神祭]]と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == [[ファイル:Osaka-zu byobu Aranigono oharae shinji.jpg|サムネイル|住吉大社の荒和大祓神事とされる豊臣期大阪図屏風に描かれた祭の様子]] 住吉祭は、[[7月31日]]に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる[[例祭|例大祭]]を中心とした祭礼。 [[生國魂祭]]（[[生國魂神社]]）・[[天神祭]]（[[大阪天満宮]]）・'''住吉祭'''（[[住吉大社]]）と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]のひとつ。 おはらいとも呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：[[祓|おはらい]]）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の[[海の日]]に、[[住吉公園]]で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（[[無形文化財]]）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。

'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼(夏越祓神事、神輿渡御祭、荒和大祓神事)。[[愛染まつり]]、[[天神祭]]と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == [[ファイル:Osaka-zu byobu Aranigono oharae shinji.jpg|サムネイル|住吉大社の荒和大祓神事とされる豊臣期大阪図屏風に描かれた祭の様子]] 住吉祭は、[[7月31日]]に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる[[例祭|例大祭]]を中心とした祭礼。 [[生國魂祭]]（[[生國魂神社]]）・[[天神祭]]（[[大阪天満宮]]）・'''住吉祭'''（[[住吉大社]]）と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]のひとつ。 おはらいとも呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：[[祓|おはらい]]）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の[[海の日]]に、[[住吉公園]]で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（[[無形文化財]]）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 特別な祭 - 住吉祭]住吉大社
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしまつり}} [[Category:大阪市の祭り]] [[Category:住吉大社]] {{event-stub}}

== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 特別な祭 - 住吉祭]住吉大社

'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼(夏越祓神事、神輿渡御祭、荒和大祓神事)。[[愛染まつり]]、[[天神祭]]と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == [[File:Osaka-zu byobu Aranigono oharae shinji.jpg|サムネイル|住吉大社の荒和大祓神事とされる豊臣期大阪図屏風に描かれた祭の様子]] 住吉祭は、[[7月31日]]に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる[[例祭|例大祭]]を中心とした祭礼。 [[生國魂祭]]（[[生國魂神社]]）・[[天神祭]]（[[大阪天満宮]]）・'''住吉祭'''（[[住吉大社]]）と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]のひとつ。 おはらいとも呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：[[祓|おはらい]]）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の[[海の日]]に、[[住吉公園]]で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（[[無形文化財]]）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 特別な祭 - 住吉祭]住吉大社

狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：[[祓|おはらい]]）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の[[海の日]]に、[[住吉公園]]で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（[[無形文化財]]）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事

'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼(夏越祓神事、神輿渡御祭、荒和大祓神事)。[[愛染まつり]]、[[天神祭]]と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == [[File:Osaka-zu byobu Aranigono oharae shinji.jpg|サムネイル|住吉大社の荒和大祓神事とされる豊臣期大阪図屏風に描かれた祭の様子]] 住吉祭は、[[7月31日]]に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる[[例祭|例大祭]]を中心とした祭礼。 [[生國魂祭]]（[[生國魂神社]]）・[[天神祭]]（[[大阪天満宮]]）・'''住吉祭'''（[[住吉大社]]）と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]のひとつ。 おはらいとも呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：[[祓|おはらい]]）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の[[海の日]]に、[[住吉公園]]で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（[[無形文化財]]）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 特別な祭 - 住吉祭]住吉大社
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしまつり}} [[Category:大阪市の祭り]] [[Category:住吉大社]] {{event-stub}}

'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼(夏越祓神事、神輿渡御祭、荒和大祓神事)。[[愛染まつり]]、[[天神祭]]と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=住吉大社の荒和大祓神事とされる豊臣期大阪図屏風に描かれた祭の様子|thumb]] 住吉祭は、[[7月31日]]に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる[[例祭|例大祭]]を中心とした祭礼。 [[生國魂祭]]（[[生國魂神社]]）・[[天神祭]]（[[大阪天満宮]]）・'''住吉祭'''（[[住吉大社]]）と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]のひとつ。 おはらいとも呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：[[祓|おはらい]]）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の[[海の日]]に、[[住吉公園]]で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（[[無形文化財]]）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 特別な祭 - 住吉祭]住吉大社
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしまつり}} [[Category:大阪市の祭り]] [[Category:住吉大社]] {{event-stub}}

== 概要 == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=住吉大社の荒和大祓神事とされる豊臣期大阪図屏風に描かれた祭の様子|thumb]] 住吉祭は、[[7月31日]]に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる[[例祭|例大祭]]を中心とした祭礼。 [[生國魂祭]]（[[生國魂神社]]）・[[天神祭]]（[[大阪天満宮]]）・'''住吉祭'''（[[住吉大社]]）と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]のひとつ。 おはらいとも呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：[[祓|おはらい]]）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の[[海の日]]に、[[住吉公園]]で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（[[無形文化財]]）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事

現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の[[海の日]]に、[[住吉公園]]で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（[[無形文化財]]）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。

revision.text:
'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼(夏越祓神事、神輿渡御祭、荒和大祓神事)。[[愛染まつり]]、[[天神祭]]と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=住吉大社の荒和大祓神事とされる豊臣期大阪図屏風に描かれた祭の様子|thumb]] 住吉祭は、[[7月31日]]に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる[[例祭|例大祭]]を中心とした祭礼。 [[生國魂祭]]（[[生國魂神社]]）・[[天神祭]]（[[大阪天満宮]]）・'''住吉祭'''（[[住吉大社]]）と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]のひとつ。 おはらいとも呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：[[祓|おはらい]]）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の[[海の日]]に、[[住吉公園]]で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（[[無形文化財]]）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事

'''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ({{ill|Kō|ja|講}}) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}}) held on {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}} at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭}} (at {{ill|Namikuni-dama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社}}) and the {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|ja|天神祭}} (at {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu|ja|大阪天満宮}}). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on {{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}, the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事}}), and the annual festival on {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from {{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}} in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Park|ja|住吉公園}}. On {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}, the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財}}) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}} are presented. On {{ill|August 1|ja|8月1日}}, a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.

'''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ({{ill|Kō|ja|講}}) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}}) held on {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}} at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭}} (at {{ill|Namikuni-dama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社}}) and the {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|ja|天神祭}} (at {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu|ja|大阪天満宮}}). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on {{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}, the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事}}), and the annual festival on {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from {{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}} in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). '''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ({{ill|Kō|ja|講}}) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}}) held on {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}} at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭}} (at {{ill|Namikuni-dama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社}}) and the {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|ja|天神祭}} (at {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu|ja|大阪天満宮}}). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on {{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}, the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事}}), and the annual festival on {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from {{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}} in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Park|ja|住吉公園}}. On {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}, the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財}}) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}} are presented. On {{ill|August 1|ja|8月1日}}, a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|住吉大社神代記" to "|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles"
revision.text:
'''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ({{ill|Kō|ja|講}}) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}}) held on {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}} at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭}} (at {{ill|Namikuni-dama Shrine|ja|生國魂神社}}) and the {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|ja|天神祭}} (at {{ill|Osaka Tenmangu|ja|大阪天満宮}}). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on {{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}, the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事}}), and the annual festival on {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from {{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}} in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Park|ja|住吉公園}}. On {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}, the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財}}) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}} are presented. On {{ill|August 1|ja|8月1日}}, a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
== External Links == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 Special Festival – Sumiyoshi Matsuri] at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}

On {{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日|12=draft}}, the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財|12=draft}}) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=draft}} are presented. On {{ill|August 1|ja|8月1日|12=draft}}, a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
== External Links == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 Special Festival – Sumiyoshi Matsuri] at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''住吉祭'''（すみよしまつり）は、7月31日に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる例大祭を中心とした祭礼(夏越祓神事、神輿渡御祭、荒和大祓神事)。[[愛染まつり]]、[[天神祭]]と共に[[大阪三大夏祭り]]の一つ。
== 概要 == [[ファイル:Osaka-zu byobu Aranigono oharae shinji.jpg|サムネイル|住吉大社の荒和大祓神事とされる豊臣期大阪図屏風に描かれた祭の様子]] 住吉祭は、[[7月31日]]に[[大阪市]]の[[住吉大社]]で行われる[[例祭|例大祭]]を中心とした祭礼。 [[生國魂祭]]（[[生國魂神社]]）・[[天神祭]]（[[大阪天満宮]]）・'''住吉祭'''（[[住吉大社]]）と続く[[大阪三大夏祭り]]のひとつ。 おはらいとも呼ばれ、住吉大社の夏祭りの一部、あるいは夏祭りそのものを指している。
狭義では、住吉大社の夏祭りのうち、7月30日に行われる宵宮祭、7月31日に行われる夏越祓神事、例大祭を指す。 広義では、7月の[[海の日]]から8月1日まで行われる住吉大社の夏祭り、そのものを指している。 土地の者たち（地元庶民）が、住吉祭と言った場合、7月31日と8月1日の住吉大社の夏祭り（夏越祓神事、例大祭、神輿渡御祭）を指す。 (住吉大社HPでは、狭義の意味で説明している）
『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、住吉大社の夏祭り（別名：[[祓|おはらい]]）は、奈良時代には、六月の御解除（みはらえ、別名:南祭）として恒例的に行なわれていた。
現在の住吉大社の夏祭りは、7月の[[海の日]]に、[[住吉公園]]で神輿洗神事が行われ、始まる。
7月31日には、夏越祓神事（[[無形文化財]]）や、第一本宮で祭典（例大祭）が行なわれ、熊野舞や[[住吉踊り]]が披露される。
8月1日には神輿渡御祭が行われる。2005年から徒歩でのお渡りが復活した。
== 日程 == *7月海の日 神輿洗神事[https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）] *7月30日 住吉祭 宵宮祭 *7月31日 住吉祭 例大祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA （動画）]、夏越祓神事 *8月1日 渡御祭[https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ （動画）]、頓宮祭、荒和大祓神事
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 特別な祭 - 住吉祭]住吉大社
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしまつり}} [[Category:大阪市の祭り]] [[Category:住吉大社]] {{event-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ([[:en:Kō (lecture)|Kō]]) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}}) held on [[:en:July 31|July 31]] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|qq=}} (at [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Namikuni-dama Shrine]]) and the [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] (at [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu]]). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on [[:en:July 30|July 30]], the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|qq=}}), and the annual festival on [[:en:July 31|July 31]]. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from [[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]] in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at [[:en:Sumiyoshi Park|Sumiyoshi Park]]. On [[:en:July 31|July 31]], the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Intangible Cultural Property]]) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|qq=}} are presented. On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
== External Links == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 Special Festival – Sumiyoshi Matsuri] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]
{{translated page|ja|住吉祭|version=101780945|comment=Imported full ja history}}

On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ([[:en:Kō (lecture)|Kō]]) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}}) held on [[:en:July 31|July 31]] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|qq=}} (at [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Namikuni-dama Shrine]]) and the [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] (at [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu]]). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on [[:en:July 30|July 30]], the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|qq=}}), and the annual festival on [[:en:July 31|July 31]]. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from [[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]] in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at [[:en:Sumiyoshi Park|Sumiyoshi Park]]. On [[:en:July 31|July 31]], the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Intangible Cultural Property]]) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|qq=}} are presented. On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
== External Links == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 Special Festival – Sumiyoshi Matsuri] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]
{{translated page|ja|住吉祭|version=101780945|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ([[:en:Kō (lecture)|Kō]]) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}}) held on [[:en:July 31|July 31]] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|qq=}} (at [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Namikuni-dama Shrine]]) and the [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] (at [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu]]). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on [[:en:July 30|July 30]], the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|qq=}}), and the annual festival on [[:en:July 31|July 31]]. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from [[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]] in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at [[:en:Sumiyoshi Park|Sumiyoshi Park]]. On [[:en:July 31|July 31]], the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Intangible Cultural Property]]) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|qq=}} are presented. On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
== External Links == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 Special Festival – Sumiyoshi Matsuri] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:大阪市の祭り]] into [[Category:Festivals of Osaka City]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ([[:en:Kō (lecture)|Kō]]) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 例祭}}) held on [[:en:July 31|July 31]] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|qq=}} (at [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Namikuni-dama Shrine]]) and the [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] (at [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu]]). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on [[:en:July 30|July 30]], the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and the annual festival on [[:en:July 31|July 31]]. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from [[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]] in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at [[:en:Sumiyoshi Park|Sumiyoshi Park]]. On [[:en:July 31|July 31]], the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Intangible Cultural Property]]) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|qq=}} are presented. On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
== External Links == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 Special Festival – Sumiyoshi Matsuri] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]
{{translated page|ja|住吉祭|version=101780945|comment=Imported full ja history}}

On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11381874]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ([[:en:Kō (lecture)|Kō]]) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}) held on [[:en:July 31|July 31]] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|qq=|1=Ikutama Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574728}} (at [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Namikuni-dama Shrine]]) and the [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] (at [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu]]). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on [[:en:July 30|July 30]], the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and the annual festival on [[:en:July 31|July 31]]. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from [[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]] in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at [[:en:Sumiyoshi Park|Sumiyoshi Park]]. On [[:en:July 31|July 31]], the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Intangible Cultural Property]]) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|qq=|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}} are presented. On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
== External Links == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 Special Festival – Sumiyoshi Matsuri] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]

It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|1=Ikutama Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574728}} (at [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Namikuni-dama Shrine]]) and the [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] (at [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu]]). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on [[:en:July 30|July 30]], the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and the annual festival on [[:en:July 31|July 31]]. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from [[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]] in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)

On [[:en:July 31|July 31]], the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Intangible Cultural Property]]) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}} are presented. == Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Matsuri]][[ja:住吉祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381874]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ([[:en:Kō (lecture)|Kō]]) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}) held on [[:en:July 31|July 31]] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|1=Ikutama Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574728}} (at [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Namikuni-dama Shrine]]) and the [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] (at [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu]]). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on [[:en:July 30|July 30]], the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and the annual festival on [[:en:July 31|July 31]]. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from [[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]] in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at [[:en:Sumiyoshi Park|Sumiyoshi Park]]. On [[:en:July 31|July 31]], the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Intangible Cultural Property]]) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}} are presented. On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony

== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}) held on [[:en:July 31|July 31]] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|1=Ikutama Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574728}} (at [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Namikuni-dama Shrine]]) and the [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] (at [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu]]). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on [[:en:July 30|July 30]], the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and the annual festival on [[:en:July 31|July 31]]. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from [[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]] in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at [[:en:Sumiyoshi Park|Sumiyoshi Park]]. On [[:en:July 31|July 31]], the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Intangible Cultural Property]]) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}} are presented. On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony

According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at [[:en:Sumiyoshi Park|Sumiyoshi Park]]. On [[:en:July 31|July 31]], the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Intangible Cultural Property]]) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}} are presented. On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony

It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ([[:en:Kō (lecture)|Kō]]) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}) held on [[:en:July 31|July 31]] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|1=Ikutama Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574728}} (at [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Namikuni-dama Shrine]]) and the [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] (at [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu]]). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on [[:en:July 30|July 30]], the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and the annual festival on [[:en:July 31|July 31]]. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from [[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]] in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Matsuri]][[ja:住吉祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Matsuri]][[ja:住吉祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Matsuri]][[ja:住吉祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381874]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ([[:en:Kō (lecture)|Kō]]) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}) held on [[:en:July 31|July 31]] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]]. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|1=Ikutama Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574728}} (at [[:en:Ikukunitama Shrine|Namikuni-dama Shrine]]) and the [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] (at [[:en:Osaka Tenmangū|Osaka Tenmangu]]). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on [[:en:July 30|July 30]], the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and the annual festival on [[:en:July 31|July 31]]. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from [[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]] in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at [[:en:Sumiyoshi Park|Sumiyoshi Park]]. On [[:en:July 31|July 31]], the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an [[:en:Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Intangible Cultural Property]]) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}} are presented. On [[:en:August 1|August 1]], a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
== External Links == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 Special Festival – Sumiyoshi Matsuri] at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]
{{translated page|ja|住吉祭|version=101780945|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Matsuri]][[ja:住吉祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Matsuri]][[ja:住吉祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Matsuri]][[ja:住吉祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381874]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ({{ill|Kō (lecture)|en|Kō (lecture)|WD=Q11632184|lt=Kō}}) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}) held on {{ill|July 31|en|July 31|WD=Q2715|lt=July 31}} at {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}} in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|1=Ikutama Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574728}} (at {{ill|Ikukunitama Shrine|en|Ikukunitama Shrine|WD=Q11574477|lt=Namikuni-dama Shrine}}) and the {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|Tenjin Matsuri|WD=Q5371810|lt=Tenjin Matsuri}} (at {{ill|Osaka Tenmangū|en|Osaka Tenmangū|WD=Q385793|lt=Osaka Tenmangu}}). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on {{ill|July 30|en|July 30|WD=Q2716|lt=July 30}}, the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and the annual festival on {{ill|July 31|en|July 31|WD=Q2715|lt=July 31}}. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from {{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|WD=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day}} in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Park|en|Sumiyoshi Park|WD=Q7637168|lt=Sumiyoshi Park}}. On {{ill|July 31|en|July 31|WD=Q2715|lt=July 31}}, the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q2901957|lt=Intangible Cultural Property}}) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}} are presented. On {{ill|August 1|en|August 1|WD=Q2788|lt=August 1}}, a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
== External Links == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 Special Festival – Sumiyoshi Matsuri] at {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}
{{translated page|ja|住吉祭|version=101780945|comment=Imported full ja history}}
