[[ja:住吉祭]] [[ja:%E4%BD%8F%E5%90%89%E7%A5%AD]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi Matsuri}} {{wikidata link|Q11381874}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Festivals of Osaka City]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-taisha]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Matsuri]][[ja:住吉祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Matsuri]][[ja:住吉祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sumiyoshi Matsuri]][[ja:住吉祭]]
'''Sumiyoshi Matsuri''' (住吉祭) is a folk event in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} in which, on the day of {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, deities and Buddhist figures are enshrined and the community remains awake throughout the night. It is also known as "Yoi Kōshin" or "Osaru-machi". Sumiyoshi Matsuri is typically held on a village-wide basis, and the gathering ({{ill|Kō (lecture)|en|Kō (lecture)|WD=Q11632184|lt=Kō|1=Kō (lecture)}}) is referred to as either '''Kōshin-kō''', '''Kōshin-e''', or "Ohimachi", among other names.
== Overview == [[File:Osaka-zu_byobu_Aranigono_oharae_shinji.jpg|alt=An Osaka folding screen from the Toyotomi period depicting the Aranigono purification ceremony at Sumiyoshi Taisha|thumb]] Sumiyoshi Matsuri centers on the annual festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}) held on {{ill|July 31|en|July 31|WD=Q2715|lt=July 31|1=July 31}} at {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}. It is one of the three major summer festivals in Osaka, along with the {{ill|Namikuni-dama Matsuri|ja|生國魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikuni-dama Matsuri|1=Ikutama Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574728}} (at {{ill|Ikukunitama Shrine|en|Ikukunitama Shrine|WD=Q11574477|lt=Namikuni-dama Shrine|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}}) and the {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|Tenjin Matsuri|WD=Q5371810|lt=Tenjin Matsuri|1=Tenjin Matsuri}} (at {{ill|Osaka Tenmangū|en|Osaka Tenmangū|WD=Q385793|lt=Osaka Tenmangu|1=Osaka Tenmangū}}). The term Sumiyoshi Matsuri may refer either specifically to a portion of the summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha or to the entire summer festival.
In a narrow sense, it denotes the sequence of events at Sumiyoshi Taisha comprising the evening festival held on {{ill|July 30|en|July 30|WD=Q2716|lt=July 30|1=July 30}}, the summer purification ceremony ({{ill|Nageharae Shinji|ja|夏越祓神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nageharae Shinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and the annual festival on {{ill|July 31|en|July 31|WD=Q2715|lt=July 31|1=July 31}}. In a broader sense, it refers to the entire summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha that takes place from {{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|WD=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day|1=Marine Day}} in July until August 1. When locals refer to "Sumiyoshi Matsuri," they typically mean the Sumiyoshi Taisha summer festival events on July 31 and August 1 (i.e. the summer purification ceremony, the annual festival, and the mikoshi procession). (The Sumiyoshi Taisha official website explains the festival in the narrow sense.)
According to the ''{{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindai-ki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}'', the summer festival of Sumiyoshi Taisha (also known as "Oharae") was regularly held during the Nara period as the "Miharai" (purification ritual), also known as the "Minamatsuri", in June.
The current summer festival at Sumiyoshi Taisha begins on Marine Day in July with a mikoshi washing ceremony at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Park|en|Sumiyoshi Park|WD=Q7637168|lt=Sumiyoshi Park|1=Sumiyoshi Park}}. On {{ill|July 31|en|July 31|WD=Q2715|lt=July 31|1=July 31}}, the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q2901957|lt=Intangible Cultural Property|1=Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}} are presented. On {{ill|August 1|en|August 1|WD=Q2788|lt=August 1|1=August 1}}, a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
== External Links == * [http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor02 Special Festival – Sumiyoshi Matsuri] at {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}
{{translated page|ja|住吉祭|version=101780945|comment=Imported full ja history}}

On {{ill|July 31|en|July 31|WD=Q2715|lt=July 31|1=July 31}}, the summer purification ceremony (an event designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q2901957|lt=Intangible Cultural Property|1=Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}) and the annual festival at the main shrine are held, during which performances such as the Kumano Dance and the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}} are presented. On {{ill|August 1|en|August 1|WD=Q2788|lt=August 1|1=August 1}}, a mikoshi procession takes place. Since 2005, the mikoshi has once again been carried on foot.
== Schedule == * July (Marine Day): Mikoshi Washing Ceremony [https://www.youtube.com/embed/6mg4r8AJjSc?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)] * July 30: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Evening Festival (Yoi-matsuri) * July 31: Sumiyoshi Matsuri – Annual Festival (Reitaisai) [https://www.youtube.com/embed/73MndOUQhOA (video)] and Summer Purification Ceremony (Nageharae Shinji) * August 1: Mikoshi Procession [https://www.youtube.com/embed/B57Q6H3xj50?list=UUw08MwmQfjjZRABPX56t3aQ (video)], Tonmiya Festival, and Arawa (Aranigono) Purification Ceremony
